items.mistake: Assuming UAT guarantees good test-set performance after training.
items.fix: Treat UAT as an expressivity result; always evaluate on held-out validation/test data and apply regularization, early stopping, or more data when the train–val gap grows.
items.why: Approximation on a region does not imply the learned weights match the true data-generating function on unseen points.
items.example: Training MSE 0.01, validation MSE 2.4 on the same feature scale — stop widening the net and add dropout or collect more labels.

items.mistake: Using linear activation (or no hidden layer) and expecting UAT-level expressivity.
items.fix: Use a non-linear activation (ReLU, tanh, sigmoid) and at least one hidden layer for non-linear targets; verify the stack is not equivalent to a single linear map.
items.why: Without non-linearity, the network remains linear regardless of width.
items.example: nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(10, 100), nn.Linear(100, 1)) without activation cannot fit XOR or sin(x) beyond linear regression.

items.mistake: Equating 'deeper is always better' because depth separation papers exist.
items.fix: Compare shallow-wide and deep-narrow candidates on validation metrics and training cost; depth helps some functions but adds optimization difficulty.
items.why: UAT already allows shallow solutions; depth is an efficiency and feature-hierarchy bet, not a theorem mandate.
items.example: A 3×256-wide MLP underperforms a 128→64→32 stack on the same tabular task despite more parameters — pick by val AUC, not depth alone.

items.mistake: Ignoring compact-domain assumptions when features are unbounded or heavy-tailed.
items.fix: Scale or clip inputs to a bounded range (standardization, min–max scaling) before invoking UAT intuition; document the operating region.
items.why: Classic statements target continuous functions on compact sets; extrapolation far outside training support is not covered.
items.example: Raw income values 0–10M without log scaling — the net may behave unpredictably beyond the training max even if UAT holds on [0, 1] after scaling.
